attacking_style: Explosive short-duration attacks in final 400-500m on extreme gradients (14%+), typically from breakaways rather than GC group. Prefers very steep finishing gradients where power-to-weight advantage maximizes.
team_tactics: Operates primarily in breakaway scenarios, positioning himself 6+ minutes off GC contention to gain freedom. Team support appears minimal once in breaks—relies on individual efforts rather than organized lead-outs.
terrain_preference: Excels on extremely steep gradients (14%+) with short, explosive finishing climbs under 1km. Strong in long breakaway scenarios requiring endurance before final punch. Mountain stages suit him when freed from GC marking.
vulnerability: Bike handling in technical descents and corners—crashed twice in observed races (2019, 2021). Physical fragility shown by multiple crashes yet ability to continue racing suggests resilience but recurring exposure.
key_patterns: Targets KOM classification aggressively from breakaways; contested points even after mid-stage crash (2021).
Final 400-500m specialist on extreme gradients; won from late attack on 14% Puy de Dôme.
Crashes frequently in technical sections; two separate incidents in observed Tour stages (2019, 2021).
Operates from early breakaways 6+ minutes down; avoids GC group marking to gain tactical freedom.
Demonstrates exceptional resilience; chased back after crash to contest KOM points same stage (2021).
form_trajectory: positive: 2023 stage win shows peak climbing form with explosive finishing power on extreme gradients after 170km
